_meta.description: Encoded ScaffoldLearn framework, learning modes, anti-sycophancy gate, and Observer Layer for the memor.re Learn skill. This file is the authoritative encoded data contract — the SKILL.md file is a thin router that references it.

scaffoldLearn.description: The canonical multi-agent learning pass. NOT a menu option or a single lens — the full Act/Notice/Check/Repair/Align sequence applied across all relevant lenses, routed to canonical Council owners.

phases.label: Act
phases.description: Who acted, what changed, and where. Name the surface, branch, PR, script, hook, or agent that produced the outcome.

phases.label: Notice
phases.description: What the user, agent, test, provider, or product surface observed. Separate the user's report from tool output from agent interpretation.

phases.label: Check
phases.description: Which authority-bearing surfaces were inspected (AGENTS.md, Council specs, skills, CI workflows, tests, provider runbooks) and which remain unchecked. Name both.

phases.label: Repair
phases.description: What artifact, code, gate, CI check, runtime behavior, or process changed. Must be committed and validated — prose is not a repair.

phases.label: Align
phases.description: The accepted rule, owner, validation command, and next action. If rule already existed: classify as rule-execution failure, not net-new lesson.

ownerRouting.infra: Provider/tool/environment lessons, surface detection, session-start hooks, CI gates, plugin freshness, stack changes
ownerRouting.closer: Release sequencing, dirty-tree discipline, branch closure, blocker packets, PR gate enforcement
ownerRouting.values: Privacy, trust, consent, security control changes, user-data handling
ownerRouting.benefactor: Spend, production-resource decisions, tool plan vs usage audits, cost/benefit
ownerRouting.visionary: Product direction, UX decisions, long-term architecture, observer-layer reflection
ownerRouting.stabilizer: User cognitive load, ceremony reduction, onboarding, error surface clarity
ownerRouting.learn: Evidence packaging, ScaffoldLearn coordination, lesson registration in learn-lessons-registry.json
ownerRouting.weight-steward: Council weight changes, specialist selection, outcome-quality scoring
ownerRouting.ghosthunter: Residue cleanup, stale artifacts, dead code, stale branches

modes.description: Internal learning lenses — evidence labels, not the full ScaffoldLearn methodology. Do not present as basic learning tiers or a substitute for the complete ScaffoldLearn pass.

entries.label: Observe
entries.description: Capture evidence, name the symptom, state a tentative rule without changing durable sources. Use for one-off or low-confidence evidence.

entries.label: Document
entries.description: Convert a repeated failure, blocker, release lesson, or user correction into a durable skill, runbook, test, issue template, or handoff rule. Requires ScaffoldLearn frame + Act/Notice/Check/Repair/Align.

entries.label: Measure
entries.description: Use measured outcomes, usage, cost, validation results, or forecast error to compare whether an existing rule is working.

entries.label: Recommend
entries.description: Produce a bounded adaptive recommendation with owner, cap, stop rule, and validation. Requires measured evidence — not available on first occurrence.

entries.label: Autonomy
entries.description: NOT approved for memor.re by default. Do not silently mutate skills, weights, provider settings, budgets, hosted resources, or production behavior without explicit approval.

antiSycophancyGate.description: Required check before preserving any lesson. All four must be named before the ScaffoldLearn Align phase.

checks.label: Current evidence
checks.prompt: What is the exact observed evidence? Name the surface, command, file, table, event, screenshot, alert, PR, provider status, or user report.

checks.label: Untested assumption
checks.prompt: What are we assuming without proof? What would have to be true for the convenient answer to be wrong?

checks.label: Missing evidence
checks.prompt: Which surface would prove or disprove the hypothesis? What would a skeptic demand to see?

checks.label: Strongest opposing case
checks.prompt: What is the strongest argument against preserving this lesson now? Test it seriously before proceeding to Align.

rules: Do not reassure from proxy evidence.
Do not retreat because Dontae pushes back unless new evidence, reasoning, or constraints change the answer.
Do not invent flaws to perform rigor. If no real flaw exists, say so plainly with evidence and name what remains uncertain.
If established rule already existed: classify as rule-execution failure. No new rule is needed — the gate is missing.

observerLayer.description: Visionary-led ScaffoldLearn checkpoint. Separates what appeared from what it means. Decides what becomes durable so memor.re can support any surface, any memory, any time, any place, any integration without flattening human experience into false authority.

collapseGuard.description: Learn must not collapse memory, thought, belief, feeling, narrative, projection, and later reflection into one accepted truth.
rules: A captured statement can be meaningful without becoming source truth, accepted memory, runtime authority, or user identity.
An AI summary is not source truth.
A feeling is not a fact, but it is still meaningful context.
A source packet is not yet accepted memory.
A local database row is not a user belief.

durabilityRules: Durable lessons must land in a canonical source future chats read: AGENTS.md, Council specs, skills, tests, or repo workflows. Local-only chat edits are not learned until committed and routed through the release path.
Fresh-chat amnesia is a workflow failure, not a memory system failure.
Prose is not a gate. A lesson without a code gate, validator, CI check, or test is prose_only and must be marked as such in learn-lessons-registry.json.
Rule execution failures do not need new rules. They need code gates that prevent the existing rule from being skipped.
Generated-artifact validation is not parallel-safe. Run build before validate.
Write → commit discipline: new lessons written locally must also be committed to the repo in the same session.
